summary.intent: Audit and fix hardcoded light-mode colors in CheckpointDetail.tsx, CheckpointTimeline.tsx, ToolUseItem.tsx, and CheckpointMessageItem.tsx to ensure proper dark mode support.
summary.outcome: All 8 hardcoded color values across 3 files were replaced with Mantine light-dark() calls; CheckpointTimeline.tsx was confirmed clean with no issues.

repo: The project uses Mantine's light-dark() CSS function for theme-aware colors rather than conditional class names or CSS variables.
Dark mode color mappings follow a pattern: light gray-0/gray-1 → dark-6/dark-7, borders gray-3 → dark-5, teal accents teal-2 → teal-8.
CheckpointTimeline.tsx uses only Mantine component props (color, c='dimmed') and avoids inline styles entirely — a clean pattern to follow.
A hardcoded #fff was present in AssistantCard background, indicating this component may not have gone through a prior dark mode pass.

code.finding: SummaryCard used teal-0 bg and teal-2 border; header bar used gray-0 bg and gray-3 border — all fixed with light-dark() equivalents.
DiffView blocks already used light-dark() correctly; only the row container bottom border (gray-1) needed fixing.
5 issues: ToolUseRow border (gray-1), UserCard bg (indigo-0), AssistantCard bg (hardcoded #fff), AssistantCard tool divider (gray-1), CompactRow border (gray-0).

workflow: Systematic tabular audit before applying fixes is effective: enumerate all issues first, then batch-apply edits.
DiffView components were already patched in a prior session — verify existing work before re-patching to avoid conflicts.

friction: The prior session patched ToolUseItem.tsx and CheckpointMessageItem.tsx but left verification incomplete, requiring re-reading and re-auditing those files.
Multiple sequential edits were needed for CheckpointMessageItem.tsx (5 separate Edit calls) due to the number of scattered hardcoded values.
open_items: The transcript lists app/src/main/index.ts, app/src/renderer/src/api.ts, app/src/renderer/src/screens/SessionDetail.tsx, and src/server.ts as modified files, but none were discussed in the audit — their changes are unaccounted for and may need review.
A broader codebase-wide grep for hardcoded color values (#fff, #000, light Mantine color tokens used without light-dark()) has not been performed and may surface additional issues.
